primary.notes: Calibration fixture with a single short_take draft. Strong on hook (LI-3), grounding (LI-2), and bracket-fit + hashtag count (LI-5); slight Linkedin-AI-cadence bleed in the 'Lesson:' close (LI-4); LI-6 cannot be meaningfully judged with N=1 and is reported at neutral midpoint with explicit caveat. Structural gate passes on length (785 ∈ [500,900]), hashtag count (3 ∈ [3,5]), and required META block; grounding gate passes — entity named, numbers attributed, no HARD-FLOOR unnamed-lived-work violation.

per_criterion.rationale: First-person, story-led opening ("Last quarter I spent two weeks watching..."). Register is observational and lived-work, not contrarian — 'By the third day I realized...' is the 'I noticed this' cadence the rubric rewards over 'you're doing this wrong.' Plain language throughout; the one quasi-technical phrase ('lens catalog') is unpacked inline as '149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames'. No Twitter-translated sharps; no aggressive declaratives. Closes on a measured 'Lesson:' rather than a hot take. Falls just short of 5/5 because the closing 'good enough to ship' tilts slightly toward declarative authority rather than continued story register, but well within thoughtful-authority territory.

SOURCE claims are concrete and traceable — '149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames' is attributed in META as 'gofreddy lens catalog v2 locked 2026-04-23', verifiable from the project's own catalog. The lived-work claim names the entity ('gofreddy', the author's own company), satisfying the HARD FLOOR check rather than tripping it. The LinkedIn-specific 'unnamed-entity vague specific' cap-at-7 doesn't trigger because the entity *is* named. INTERPRETIVE claim ('your audit is good enough...') is correctly framed as JR's takeaway. Held back from 9-10 because the 'About page describes the company in three substantively different ways across three pages' is a specific factual claim that a buyer cannot verify in 2 minutes without seeing the pages — minor specificity-without-receipt friction.

Hook is 95 chars: 'Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch.' Well under the ~210 desktop show-more cutoff. Story-led, specific (named product, time window, sensory detail of 'finish before lunch' implying speed), and earns line two via the 'and then I noticed something' pull of the second sentence ('By the third day I realized it was scoring my own positioning'). No contrarian declarative, no generic 'Are you ready for...' bait, no engagement-bait close. Falls short of 5/5 because the hook signals 'speed of the tool' more than the actual lesson (self-audit coherence) — a tighter version would foreshadow the dogfooding twist before the show-more break.

Deterministic AI-tell scan: no 'Game-changer', no 'Here's what I learned' alone-line close, no 'Thoughts? 👇', no emoji, no excessive newline padding (paragraph breaks serve structure). However, the closing 'Lesson: when your audit is good enough...' is a mild LinkedIn-AI-adjacent pattern — the colon-then-takeaway scaffold is one of the formulaic transition markers the rubric flags ('here's the thing' family). Voice is mostly JR but the 'Lesson:' label reads as a slight thought-leadership cadence bleed. One pattern, not multiple — fits the LI-4 score-3 description ('one or two LinkedIn-AI patterns slip through') translated to the 0-10 scale as 7.

SHORT_TAKE bracket declared (500-900); actual char_count 785 sits comfortably mid-range. Structure matches the SHORT_TAKE spec: story-opening (sentence 1) + 1 substantive paragraph (the realization + the audit's specifics) + closing thought ('Lesson:...'). Three targeted hashtags (B2BMarketing, MarketingAudit, AIagents) — exactly inside the 3-5 ideal range, no penalty. Hashtags map to JR's brand pillars (B2B marketing, the marketing-audit product, AI agents) rather than generic '#marketing'. Not pad-to-length: every paragraph carries content. Falls short of 9-10 because the middle paragraph could compress — 'The same scoring that would tell a fintech founder where their conversion funnel leaks was telling me my own brand narrative was incoherent' restates the parallel that the prior paragraph already established.

Cohort contains a single draft (drafts/short_take.md). Cross-item archetype variance and pillar-spread cannot be meaningfully evaluated on N=1 — the geometric mean of one per-draft cohort-fit score is degenerate. Scored at the neutral midpoint to avoid penalizing a calibration fixture for not providing a cohort, while flagging the limitation. The single draft uses a story-led archetype on the B2B-marketing pillar; whether that's part of a varied cohort is unobservable here.

secondary.notes: Strong LinkedIn short take overall. Main weaknesses are unverifiable grounding due to missing voice.md/reference artifacts and jargon that is not fully translated into plain English.

per_criterion.rationale: Strong first-person, story-led LinkedIn voice: 'Last quarter I spent two weeks...' and 'By the third day I realized...' feel like lived-work reflection, not a hot take. The tone is thoughtful and buyer-accessible in places. However, '149 always-on lenses, 25 vertical bundles, 9 Phase-0 meta-frames' introduces jargon without a clear plain-English follow-up, triggering the rubric cap.

The draft makes specific lived-work claims about gofreddy, the lens catalog, '149 always-on lenses,' '25 vertical bundles,' and '9 Phase-0 meta-frames.' The named entity gofreddy appears in the draft, but no programs/references/voice.md artifact is provided here, so the required grounding cannot be verified from the supplied artifacts. Claims are plausible and internally consistent, but not fact-checkable from what is in front of me.

The opener is story-led and concrete: 'Last quarter I spent two weeks watching gofreddy's marketing audit run finish before lunch.' It establishes time, subject, and lived-work context before the cutoff. It earns the next line without sounding contrarian or bait-y.

No obvious LinkedIn-AI tells like 'Thoughts?', emoji prompts, fake hot-take framing, or whitespace padding. The close begins with 'Lesson:', which is somewhat formulaic, but it is not the banned standalone 'Here's what I learned' cadence and it lands as a concise implication.

The SHORT_TAKE structure is appropriate: story opening, substantive middle, and a clear closing thought. It has 3 targeted hashtags in metadata: 'B2BMarketing, MarketingAudit, AIagents.' The structure earns its 785 characters and does not feel padded.

Only one draft is provided in the session artifacts, so cohort variation cannot be demonstrated. The single draft is story-led and uses one voice_pillar, 'B2B-marketing.' Scoring only what is in front of me, the cohort does not show archetype or pillar spread.
